items.front: 9-2-8: Controller responsibility for special interest sites
items.back: NO responsibility to monitor or observe unless directed by supervisory/CIC. But immediately RELAY any reports of unusual activity to supervisory/CIC.
14 CFR Part 93 — Special Air Traffic Rules. Not all part 93 areas are SFRAs, but rules apply to all SFRA-designated areas.
Two-way radio communications, flight planning, and an operational transponder on an assigned code.

items.front: 9-2-10: What to do for loss of comm or inability to track in SFRA?
items.back: Immediately report to OS/CIC and WAIT for instructions.
Non-landing: squawk VFR, frequency change approved upon exit. Landing inside area: remain on assigned transponder code until after landing.

items.front: 9-2-10: Who is exempt from flight plan filing in DC SFRA?
items.back: U.S. military, law enforcement, and aeromedical flights.
Inflight identification, surveillance, interdiction, and pursuit by government aircraft for national defense, homeland security, and intelligence/law enforcement purposes.

items.front: 9-2-12: Three prohibitions for sensitive govt mission aircraft
items.back: Do NOT: change sensitive beacon codes, alter/abbreviate U.S. special call signs, or fail to enter full call sign in automation to max extent possible.

items.front: 9-2-12: What to do with stolen aircraft information?
items.back: Forward to OS/CIC for reporting on the Domestic Events Network (DEN).

items.front: 9-2-13: When does MARSA end during aerial refueling?
items.back: When tanker advises aircraft are vertically positioned in refueling airspace AND ATC advises MARSA is terminated.
Altitude or course changes issued after MARSA is declared but prior to rendezvous.

items.front: 9-2-13: Two-way comm failure — tanker vs receiver departure altitudes
items.back: Tanker departs track from HIGHEST altitude in block. Receiver departs from LOWEST altitude. Both squawk 7600 for ≥2 min before departing.

items.front: 9-2-13: Nonradar routes for aerial refueling — when allowed?
items.back: Only after refueling aircraft have passed the ARCP. Vectors/altitude changes may be furnished at any time.

items.front: 9-2-14: 16-hour rule for supersonic flight plans above FL 600
items.back: Flight plans involving supersonic flight are required 16 hours in advance. Originating ARTCC may waive this requirement.
At least one high altitude fix per ARTCC area. Entry/exit points of turns ≥90° must be designated. Elapsed times from takeoff to first fix in each ARTCC required.

items.front: 9-2-14: Lateral airspace protection for turns above FL 600
items.back: Supersonic: 75 NM overflown side / 25 NM other side. Subsonic: 34 NM overflown side / 25 NM other side. Straight flight: 25 NM either side.
5,000 feet (in lieu of Chapter 4 normal value).
